description_long: This is a traditional push broom with a long, straight wooden handle and a wide, rectangular broom head. The handle appears to be made of polished wood, showing a natural brown hue with subtle grain patterns. The broom head is attached firmly to the handle and features a dense row of dark bristles, likely synthetic or natural fiber, arranged in a straight line. The bristles are tightly packed and extend evenly from the base, designed for sweeping large flat surfaces such as sidewalks or garage floors. The end of the handle opposite the bristles is plain, with no visible grip enhancements or hanging holes. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for heavy-duty urban cleaning tasks.
description: A classic push broom with a long wooden handle and a wide, densely bristled head, ideal for sweeping large urban surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the wide, rectangular broom head with dense bristles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the broom head and the straight alignment of the bristles.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the handle and the back of the broom head with bristles visible below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile of the broom head and the straight bristle arrangement.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, round wooden handle with a smooth finish and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: Bristles are densely packed in a straight row along the wide edge of the broom head, uniform in length and dark in color.
attribute_mounting_position: Intended to be stored vertically by leaning against a wall; no visible mounting hole or hook.
